title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary:
Looking at the review again more carefully to ensure accuracy:

**Summary:** The reviewer's single blocking issue is that the PR adds a new policy-validation feature (new `PolicyValidReady` condition, validation Job, gating behavior) but no Chainsaw E2E test, breaking the project's pattern where all 21 existing features have dedicated E2E tests. They require creating `tests/e2e/keystone/policy-validation/chainsaw-test.yaml` covering condition transitions, Job existence, and deployment gating. Four additional warnings were raised but their content was truncated in the provided review.

<promise>IMPROVEMENT_COMPLETE</promise>
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 3a5c8bd-dirty with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=1 critical=0 warning=4 info=3 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## BLOCKING (1)

### B-001: Missing Chainsaw E2E test for policy-validation feature
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Enforce consistent patterns across sibling functions

**Problem**: The project has 21 Chainsaw E2E test directories under tests/e2e/keystone/ covering every existing feature. This PR introduces a new feature (policy validation gating) with a new condition (PolicyValidReady), a new Job resource, and new gating behavior but adds no Chainsaw E2E test. The existing policy-overrides/ E2E test (CC-0016) only verifies ConfigMap content and does not assert PolicyValidReady condition status, validation Job existence, or gating behavior.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/
├── policy-overrides/      # CC-0016: only checks ConfigMap content
├── trust-flush/           # CC-0057: has dedicated E2E test
├── upgrade-flow/          # CC-0056: has dedicated E2E test
└── (NO policy-validation/ directory)
```

**Action Required**: Create tests/e2e/keystone/policy-validation/ with a chainsaw-test.yaml that deploys a Keystone CR with policyOverrides set, asserts PolicyValidReady transitions through False/PolicyValidationInProgress to True/PolicyValidationPassed, asserts the validation Job exists with the correct name pattern, and verifies DeploymentReady only appears after PolicyValidReady=True.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Create tests/e2e/keystone/policy-validation/chainsaw-test.yaml that: 1) Deploys a Keystone CR with policyOverrides set, 2) Asserts PolicyValidReady transitions through False/PolicyValidationInProgress → True/PolicyValidationPassed, 3) Asserts the validation Job exists with the correct name pattern, 4) Verifies DeploymentReady only appears after PolicyValidReady=True.
```

---

## WARNING (4)

### W-001: Missing PropagationPolicy: Background on Job deletion
**File**: `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:184-191` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: New helper functions must follow sibling patterns in the same package

**Problem**: deleteValidationJob deletes a Job without specifying PropagationPolicy. The shared job.RunJob() function in internal/common/job/job.go:82 explicitly uses DeletePropagationBackground with a detailed comment (CC-0056) explaining this is needed for envtest consistency. The deleteValidationJob function could leave orphaned Pods in envtest scenarios.

**Code**:
```
func deleteValidationJob(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, namespace, name string) error {
	j := &batchv1.Job{}
	j.SetName(name)
	j.SetNamespace(namespace)
	if err := client.IgnoreNotFound(c.Delete(ctx, j)); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("deleting Job %s/%s: %w", namespace, name, err)
	}
	return nil
}
```

**Action Required**: Add DeletePropagationBackground to the Delete call, matching the pattern established in the shared RunJob function.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
func deleteValidationJob(ctx context.Context, c client.Client, namespace, name string) error {
	j := &batchv1.Job{}
	j.SetName(name)
	j.SetNamespace(namespace)
	propagation := metav1.DeletePropagationBackground
	if err := client.IgnoreNotFound(c.Delete(ctx, j, &client.DeleteOptions{PropagationPolicy: &propagation})); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("deleting Job %s/%s: %w", namespace, name, err)
	}
	return nil
}
```

---

### W-002: Condition strings not extracted into typed constants
**File**: `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:42-80` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Extract repeated condition strings into typed constants

**Problem**: The condition type "PolicyValidReady" and reasons "NotRequired", "PolicyValidationInProgress", "PolicyValidationPassed", "PolicyValidationFailed" appear as string literals in multiple places across reconcile_policyvalidation.go (lines 42, 59, 64, 80) and are repeated in tests. Sibling reconcilers (e.g. reconcile_networkpolicy.go lines 29-33) have already established the pattern of extracting condition strings into typed constants.

**Code**:
```
conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
    Type:   "PolicyValidReady",
    Reason: "NotRequired",
    ...
conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
    Type:   "PolicyValidReady",
    Reason: "PolicyValidationFailed",
    ...
conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
    Type:   "PolicyValidReady",
    Reason: "PolicyValidationInProgress",
    ...
conditions.SetCondition(&keystone.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
    Type:   "PolicyValidReady",
    Reason: "PolicyValidationPassed",
```

**Action Required**: Extract all condition type and reason strings into typed constants at the top of the file and update all usages.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
const (
	conditionTypePolicyValidReady            = "PolicyValidReady"
	conditionReasonNotRequired               = "NotRequired"
	conditionReasonPolicyValidationInProgress = "PolicyValidationInProgress"
	conditionReasonPolicyValidationPassed     = "PolicyValidationPassed"
	conditionReasonPolicyValidationFailed     = "PolicyValidationFailed"
)
```

**Related**: I-001

---

### W-003: Missing resources field propagation to validation Job
**File**: `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:152-160` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Verify new fields propagate to all applicable resource types

**Problem**: The acceptance criteria states the validation Job should use the same image, SecurityContext, and config mounts as the API Deployment. The Job correctly uses restrictedSecurityContext() and the config volume mount, but does not propagate spec.resources (resource requests/limits). Sibling Job builders (buildDBJob in reconcile_database.go) face the same gap, tracked with TODO comments in CC-0042.

**Code**:
```
Containers: []corev1.Container{{
    Name:  "validator",
    Image: fmt.Sprintf("%s:%s", keystone.Spec.Image.Repository, keystone.Spec.Image.Tag),
    Command: []string{
        "oslopolicy-checker",
        "--namespace", "keystone",
        "--policy", "/etc/keystone/keystone.conf.d/policy.yaml",
    },
    SecurityContext:          restrictedSecurityContext(),
    TerminationMessagePolicy: corev1.TerminationMessageFallbackToLogsOnError,
```

**Action Required**: Either propagate keystone.Spec.Resources to the validation Job container if present, or add a TODO comment matching the existing CC-0042 pattern for consistency across all Job builders.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a TODO comment for consistency with the tracked gap, or propagate resources:
    // TODO(CC-0042): propagate keystone.Spec.Resources to validation Job container
    SecurityContext:          restrictedSecurityContext(),
```

---

### W-004: Silent error swallowing in getValidationErrorMessage pod list query
**File**: `reconcile_policyvalidation.go:103-109` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Verify all pod specs share required volume mounts

**Problem**: The getValidationErrorMessage function silently swallows the error when the Pod list query fails, returning only the fallback message. When debugging failed validation Jobs at 3am, the missing error log makes it harder to diagnose why pod status extraction failed.

**Code**:
```
if err := c.List(ctx, &pods,
    client.InNamespace(namespace),
    client.MatchingLabels{"job-name": jobName},
); err != nil || len(pods.Items) == 0 {
    return fallback
}
```

**Action Required**: Log the error before returning the fallback message to aid debugging.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
if err := c.List(ctx, &pods,
    client.InNamespace(namespace),
    client.MatchingLabels{"job-name": jobName},
); err != nil {
    log.FromContext(ctx).Error(err, "failed to list pods for validation error extraction", "job", jobName)
    return fallback
}
if len(pods.Items) == 0 {
    return fallback
}
```

---

## INFO (3)

**File**: `keystone_controller_test.go:305` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Ensure test names accurately describe their coverage scope

**Problem**: The test was updated to include PolicyValidReady in the condition list. Initial concern was whether the mock reconciler exercises the reconcilePolicyValidation path, but the testKeystone() fixture has PolicyOverrides: nil, which exercises the NotRequired path setting PolicyValidReady=True. This is acceptable since unit tests for each specific path exist separately.

**Code**:
```
for _, condType := range []string{"SecretsReady", "FernetKeysReady", "CredentialKeysReady", "DatabaseReady", "PolicyValidReady", "DeploymentReady", "HPAReady", "NetworkPolicyReady", "BootstrapReady", "TrustFlushReady"} {
```

**Action Required**: No action required. The test correctly covers the nil-policy path. Separate unit tests cover the PolicyValidationPassed and PolicyValidationFailed paths.

**Suggested Fix**:

No changes needed. Verified correct.

### I-003: Test coverage is comprehensive for unit and integration tests
**File**: `reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: This is a positive observation, not a problem. The PR includes 16 unit tests covering all paths (nil policy, Job cleanup, Job created, Job running, Job complete, Job failed, error extraction, fallback message, stale Job detection, Job spec validation, and ObservedGeneration), 2 integration tests verifying gating behavior, Helm unit tests for RBAC, and controller-level tests updated for PolicyValidReady.

**Code**:
```
16 unit tests in reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go
2 integration tests in integration_test.go
Helm unit tests for ClusterRole and Role RBAC
Controller-level tests updated for PolicyValidReady
```

**Action Required**: No action required. Test coverage is thorough at the unit and integration level. The missing E2E test is tracked as a separate finding.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
No changes needed.
```

**Related**: B-001

---

## Summary

This is a well-structured PR that follows established codebase patterns closely. The code quality is high with solid security context, RBAC, error handling, and comprehensive unit/integration test coverage. The main gap is the missing Chainsaw E2E test — with 21 existing E2E test directories covering every other feature, this new feature with its own condition, Job, and gating behavior warrants one. Secondary concerns include inconsistent Job deletion behavior (missing PropagationPolicy: Background) and condition string literals that should be extracted into typed constants per established patterns.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 1 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 4 |
| INFO     | 3 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Do not merge until the Chainsaw E2E test is added. The WARNING-level findings (PropagationPolicy, typed constants, error logging) should also be addressed before merge, as they represent inconsistencies with established codebase patterns. The resources propagation gap can be tracked via the existing CC-0042 TODO pattern.
resolution_summary: All review findings were addressed: the blocking B-001 was resolved by creating a comprehensive Chainsaw E2E test suite in tests/e2e/keystone/policy-validation/ (4 new files: chainsaw-test.yaml, 00-policy-cm.yaml, 01-keystone-cr.yaml, 02-patch-disable-policy.yaml) covering PolicyValidReady condition transitions, validation Job existence, DeploymentReady gating, and policy removal cleanup. All four warnings were fixed in reconcile_policyvalidation.go: W-001 added DeletePropagationBackground to deleteValidationJob (line 203-204); W-002 extracted all condition string literals into typed constants (conditionTypePolicyValidReady, conditionReasonNotRequired, conditionReasonPolicyValidationInProgress, conditionReasonPolicyValidationPassed, conditionReasonPolicyValidationFailed) at lines 28-33 and updated all usages throughout the file; W-003 added a TODO(CC-0042) comment at line 166 matching the established pattern for resource propagation gaps; W-004 split the combined error/empty-pods check into separate blocks and added error logging via log.FromContext(ctx).Error() at line 115 before returning the fallback message. The W-002 constant extraction also required updating reconcile_policyvalidation_test.go to remain consistent with the production code changes.
